DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS -- JONATHAN BORNSTEIN APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY. MARK HANCOCK APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND TO THE QUESTION CONCERNING HOW MANY EMPLOYMENT CASES BEFORE 1994 HAD DEFENDANT CONDUCTED THROUGH TRIAL TO VERDICT OR JUDGMENT. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE OTHER QUESTIONS SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF MATTERS IN DISPUTE SUBMITTED PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 335. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
